behavior_summary: Provides a short examine/U8P_FISHPOLE::look description for the fishing pole (shows text "fishing pole").
When used, it suspends to obtain a player-selected target (process target()). If no target is selected, the player is told "No fish here."

If a target exists and its Item::getType equals 0x015B (inferred fishable target), a urandom(10) check (~10% chance) may trigger a catch: the script reads target coordinates, calls Item::legal_create to attempt creation of an item of type 0x0270 at/near those coords (using urandom(3) as a variant), and on success calls Item::shoot to spawn/launch the created item and barks "I caught something!".
notable_control_flow: U8P_FISHPOLE::use suspends to capture a target id (local1) from process target(); immediate guard: local1 == 0 => show bark "No fish here." and exit.
If target present, test Item::getType(target) == 0x015B and urandom(10) == 7 together gate the catch branch (chance + type guard).

On successful Item::legal_create the script reads the created item's coords and calls Item::shoot to produce a visual/physical spawn effect before barks; failure of legal_create yields no spawn and no success bark.
Locals are reused across steps (local1 initially holds target id then later used for z-values), so values are overwritten as the routine progresses.
open_questions: Exact meaning of numeric type IDs: 0x015B is inferred as the fishable target type and 0x0270 as the caught-fish item, but the canonical item definitions are not present.
Whether the created item (type 0x0270) is consumable or a persistent world item (the script only creates/shoots it, not explicitly give it to the player).
Whether process target() always represents a player-clicked world tile/object or can return non-player targets in some contexts (affects when catching is possible).
How often the urandom(10) == 7 branch triggers in practice (interpreted as ~10% chance but depends on urandom implementation and range semantics).

main_function.name: U8P_FISHPOLE::look
main_function.purpose: Handle player examine/U8P_FISHPOLE::look on the fishing pole by showing a short description string and terminating/suspending the U8P_FISHPOLE::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine when the player examines (looks at) the fishing pole item/NPC.
Runs as a U8P_FISHPOLE::look-type process (process type 0x0208) and shows the text "fishing pole" then suspends.

function_map.name: U8P_FISHPOLE::use
function_map.role: Perform fishing action: target selection, type and chance checks, attempt to create and spawn caught item, and emit success/failure barks

function_map.name: process target()
function_map.role: Engine call to obtain the currently selected/targeted process/object id

function_map.name: Item::legal_create
function_map.role: Check/create an item at coordinates (returns success and out-created-item reference)

function_map.name: Item::shoot
function_map.role: Spawn/launch the created item as a projectile/visual effect

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Random number generator used for chance/variant selection

external_calls.purpose: Suspend to obtain the currently selected/targeted object/process id; assigned to local1.
Generate random numbers for catch chance (urandom(10)) and to choose a variant/orientation for Item::legal_create (urandom(3)).
Query target's type id to check if it matches the fishable type (compared to 0x015B).
Read X coordinate of the target (used to position creation) and later read the created item's X after creation.
Read Y coordinate of the target and later the created-item Y coordinate.
Read Z/stack coordinate of the target and later the created-item Z coordinate.
Attempt to create the caught-item (type 0x0270) at/near the target coordinates with a variant from urandom(3); returns a boolean success and an out reference (local4).
Spawn/launch the created item to produce the caught-fish effect using coords and the created-item reference.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides a short examine/U8P_FISHPOLE::look description for the fishing pole (shows text "fishing pole").
